notes: Lesson 23 orientation (act 134), 'DP reactivation — the four decisions at speed', 850.32s, 8 long cues (60-127s each). The lesson map: DP is strong-but-stale, goal = recall speed (recurrence from memory < 2 min); every DP = state/recurrence/order/base in fixed order; then Kadane (running scalar), 0/1 knapsack (rolling array, descending), the ascending flip to coin change / unbounded, LIS (dp-ends-at-i O(n^2) vs patience tails O(n log n)), and the four traps. Uses dp.py (decisions_ledger, item_card, sweep_arrow, choice_pair) + arrays.py (value_row, complexity). Two iterations: (1) authored + manim -s smoke all 8 cues READ (fixed 3 bottom-note safe-area violations: to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.45) at bottom=-3.55 exceeded safe -3.50 -> buff 0.55), (2) full -ql render (all 8 chunks pinned within <=0.033s) + READ start/mid/end of every cue from the 850s muxed mp4. Per-cue checklist (concepts match narration, formulas/complexity correct O(n^2)/O(n log n)/O(nW)/O(W), LIS highlighted subsequence 1<4<5<9 strictly increasing, descending=use-once vs ascending=reuse contrast correct, safe-area guard, chunk timing pinned): ALL PASS. Note: on this long segment the per-cue _end frames (cues 02-06) land on the NEXT cue's blank opening headline — the documented keyframe-seek artifact (imprecise -ss input-seek snaps to a sparse keyframe on a 14-min video); each cue's content is confirmed by its mid frame + the -s smoke last-frame. cue07_end re-extracted at 849.4s shows the complete traps grid.
